channel.title: MIND / MATTER Daily Briefing
channel.description: New research across consciousness, quantum foundations, and neuroscience.

item.title: Spin quantum computing, spin quantum cognition
item.description: Abstract Over two decades ago, Bruce Kane proposed that spin-half phosphorus nuclei embedded in a spin-zero silicon substrate could serve as a viable platform for spin-based quantum computing. These nuclear spins exhibit remarkably long single-spin coherence times, making them ideal candidates for qubits.

item.title: The Five-Year Horizon: Quantum Computing and the Philosophy of Technical Becoming
item.description: Certain physics-based technologies (quantum computing, fusion energy, advanced materials, brain–computer interfaces) have remained “five years away” for decades. This paper argues that this perpetual horizon is not a forecasting failure but the temporal signature of Perpetual Five-Year Technologies (PFYTs): technologies of atoms, not bits, whose technical object and enabling ecosystem must co-develop.

item.title: Brain criticality characterizes abnormal neural dynamics and metabolism in disorder of consciousness
item.description: Revealing how disrupted brain dynamics lead to altered consciousness levels remains a central challenge in understanding the neural mechanisms underlying consciousness. The brain criticality framework offers a promising perspective, in which optimal neural integration and information processing occur when the brain operates near a critical point, while also reflecting fundamental neural processes such as excitation/inhibition balance.

item.title: QuantiSpect: A Structure-Aware Lightweight 3D CNN Pre-Decoder for Scalable Surface Code Quantum Error Correction
item.description: Real-time decoding is a critical bottleneck for large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computing. AI-based neural pre-decoders locally correct most physical errors before passing residual syndromes to a global decoder, enabling sub-microsecond latencies.

item.title: Consciousness in the Brain: An Integrative Review of Contemporary Theories
item.description: Understanding consciousness remains one of the most significant challenges in neuroscience, philosophy, and cognitive science. Despite substantial advances in neuroimaging, electrophysiology, and computational modeling, a comprehensive account of the neural basis of conscious experience has yet to emerge.

item.title: Advances in biomarkers for diagnosing and prognosticating disorders of consciousness
item.description: Disorders of Consciousness (DoC) resulting from brain injury comprise a spectrum of clinical syndromes, where the level of impairment varies considerably depending on lesion location, etiology, severity, and individual patient factors. These differences substantially influence both rehabilitation strategies and long-term prognosis.

item.title: Advances in local field potential research in prolonged disorders of consciousness: a narrative review
item.description: Prolonged disorders of consciousness (pDOC)-including coma, vegetative state/unresponsive wakefulness syndrome (VS/UWS), and minimally conscious state (MCS)-remain diagnostically and therapeutically challenging. Even with validated instruments such as the Coma Recovery Scale-Revised (CRS-R), behavioral assessment yields misdiagnosis rates approaching 40%, as fluctuating arousal, motor impairment, and sedation can mask preserved cognition.

item.title: The Hidden Injury: Behavioral Health's Role in Pediatric Orthopaedic Outcomes-A Systematic Review.
item.description: New work indexed under “The Hidden Injury: Behavioral Health's Role in Pediatric Orthopaedic Outcomes-A Systematic Review..” The source did not provide an abstract; read the original before evaluating its claims.

item.title: Enhanced NQS via Annealed Gradient Descent
item.description: Neural quantum states offer expressive representations of quantum many-body wave functions, yet their practical accuracy can be limited by stochastic optimization rather than representational capacity. Here we identify a finite-sample instability, termed subspace trapping, in which physically important configurations become strongly underestimated, remain absent from successive sampling batches and receive insufficient gradient feedback.

item.title: Immediate effects of sitting position on cervical and trunk posture, perceived discomfort, and RULA-based ergonomic risk during smartphone gaming: A randomized within-subject study.
item.description: New work indexed under “Immediate effects of sitting position on cervical and trunk posture, perceived discomfort, and RULA-based ergonomic risk during smartphone gaming: A randomized within-subject study..” The source did not provide an abstract; read the original before evaluating its claims.

item.title: Effect of IoC2-guided analgesic titration on intraoperative opioid consumption in older patients undergoing laparoscopic colorectal surgery: a randomized controlled trial.
item.description: New work indexed under “Effect of IoC2-guided analgesic titration on intraoperative opioid consumption in older patients undergoing laparoscopic colorectal surgery: a randomized controlled trial..” The source did not provide an abstract; read the original before evaluating its claims.

item.title: Non-pharmacological preoperative interventions for the prevention of emergence delirium in children under general anesthesia: a systematic review and network meta-analysis.
item.description: New work indexed under “Non-pharmacological preoperative interventions for the prevention of emergence delirium in children under general anesthesia: a systematic review and network meta-analysis..” The source did not provide an abstract; read the original before evaluating its claims.

item.title: Quantum consciousness revisited: a review of Orch OR and an introduction to the Quantum Cognition Gradient
item.description: Despite centuries of scientific inquiry, the origin of human consciousness, characterized by self-awareness and the presence of qualia, remains one of the deepest mysteries in neuroscience. Orchestrated Objective Reduction (Orch OR), as proposed by Roger Penrose and Stuart Hameroff, posits that consciousness arises from the objective reduction of quantum superpositions within entangled neural microtubules, triggered by gravitational self-energy.

item.title: Individual-Specific Functional Connectivity-Based State Classification and Prognosis Prediction for Disorders of Consciousness.
item.description: New work indexed under “Individual-Specific Functional Connectivity-Based State Classification and Prognosis Prediction for Disorders of Consciousness..” The source did not provide an abstract; read the original before evaluating its claims.

item.title: Equivariant Continuous Normalizing Flows with Offline Sampling for Fermionic Ground State Estimation
item.description: We introduce a framework for fermionic variational Monte Carlo (VMC) in which a continuous normalizing flow (CNF) refines a fixed antisymmetric base wavefunction. The flow is implemented as a permutation-equivariant neural ODE, a smooth, topology-preserving map that learns correlations not captured by the base; equivariance preserves the antisymmetry of the base, so the flow can in principle improve any antisymmetric ansatz that can be sampled efficiently.

item.title: The fermion sector of the SMEFT from asymptotically safe gravity
item.description: Quantum gravity impacts Standard Model fields through effective interactions generated by renormalization. These appear as Standard Model Effective Field Theory (SMEFT) operators, where the effects of new physics are parametrized by the values of the higher-order SMEFT coefficients.

item.title: Effect of intermittent abdominal ice application on postoperative pain and opioid consumption after elective cesarean section: a randomized controlled study.
item.description: New work indexed under “Effect of intermittent abdominal ice application on postoperative pain and opioid consumption after elective cesarean section: a randomized controlled study..” The source did not provide an abstract; read the original before evaluating its claims.

item.title: Ventilation heterogeneity under mechanical ventilation via mask during anesthetic induction: a randomized trial comparing two ventilation strategies.
item.description: New work indexed under “Ventilation heterogeneity under mechanical ventilation via mask during anesthetic induction: a randomized trial comparing two ventilation strategies..” The source did not provide an abstract; read the original before evaluating its claims.

item.title: Randomized Feasibility Trial of Routine Versus Selective Transesophageal Echocardiography During Isolated Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting.
item.description: New work indexed under “Randomized Feasibility Trial of Routine Versus Selective Transesophageal Echocardiography During Isolated Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting..” The source did not provide an abstract; read the original before evaluating its claims.

item.title: The regulation of GABAergic nervous system in sleep‑wake states and general anesthesia
item.description: Sleep-wake cycles and general anesthesia both involve reversible alterations in consciousness and share important neurobehavioral characteristics; however, their underlying mechanisms remain incompletely understood. As the principal inhibitory neuronal population in the central nervous system, GABAergic neurons play a key regulatory role in regulating transitions between states of consciousness.
